examples.title: Pseudocode: Ethereum Transaction
examples.description: Demonstrates the flow of an Ethereum transaction
examples.code:
ALGORITHM EthereumTransaction
INPUT: sender, recipient, amount, gasPrice, gasLimit
OUTPUT: transactionHash

BEGIN
 1. Initialize transaction object
 2. Set sender, recipient, amount, gasPrice, gasLimit
 3. Calculate transaction hash
 4. Sign transaction with sender's private key
 5. Broadcast transaction to Ethereum network
 6. Return transaction hash
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the steps involved in creating and broadcasting an Ethereum transaction.

examples.title: Python: Ethereum Transaction
examples.description: Demonstrates the flow of an Ethereum transaction using Python
examples.code:
from web3 import Web3

# Initialize Web3 provider
w3 = Web3(Web3.HTTPProvider('https://mainnet.infura.io/v3/YOUR-PROJECT-ID'))

# Set sender, recipient, amount, gasPrice, gasLimit
sender = '0x742d35Cc6634C0532925a3b844Bc9e7595f0bEb'
recipient = '0x55241586d50469745864804697458648046974'
amount = 1

gasPrice = 20

gasLimit = 100000

# Create and sign transaction
transaction = {
 'nonce': w3.eth.getTransactionCount(sender),
 'gasPrice': w3.toWei(gasPrice, 'gwei'),
 'gas': gasLimit,
 'to': recipient,
 'value': w3.toWei(amount, 'ether')
}

signed_transaction = w3.eth.account.sign_transaction(transaction, 'YOUR-PRIVATE-KEY')

# Broadcast transaction
w3.eth.sendRawTransaction(signed_transaction.rawTransaction)

# Get transaction hash
transaction_hash = w3.toHex(w3.sha3(signed_transaction.rawTransaction))

print(transaction_hash)
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the steps involved in creating and broadcasting an Ethereum transaction using the Web3 library.
